title: Lyperia antirrhinoides
intro: Lyperia antirrhinoides, also known as the Cape Antirrhinum or Cape Lyperia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. The bioactive constituents of Lyperia antirrhinoides include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Lyperia antirrhinoides belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. It is a flowering plant native to South Africa, and its classification is within the genus Lyperia. Lyperia antirrhinoides is a member of the order Lamiales. This plant is also known as the Cape foxglove or Cape foxglove flower. Further classification includes its kingdom as Plantae.
